reproQuery: { article(id:"a1"){ id title ... @defer(label:"typename_only_parent"){ __typename } reviews{ id ... @defer(label:"review_typename_author"){ __typename author{ __typename id displayName } } } } }
minimizationNote: parent-only `...@defer{__typename}` is clean; bug needs the nested in-list reviews `...@defer{__typename author{...}}` combination - exact minimal still to pin
observed: valid query (normal=full data,0 errors); defer returns a SINGLE frame {"data":{"article":null}} with NO terminal frame (hasNext absent) -> wrong data + client hang
expectedPerSpec: defer must reconstruct to the normal-mode article and emit a terminal hasNext:false frame

reproQuery: surfaced under concurrent @defer load (oracle fires normal+defer in parallel; batch of ~170 ops). Defer dispatch graphql_handler.go:354 -> resolve.go:639 errgroup spawns parallel fetches that race on the shared astjson document.
observed: 3 distinct DATA RACE reports (Go -race): goroutines from the defer errgroup concurrently WRITE (astjson mergevalues via loader.go:657) and READ (astjson parser via resolvable.go:906/inputtemplate input render, loader.go:371/1412) the SAME astjson value address without synchronization. Race line-pairs: loader.go 657/376, 657/353, 683/365.
expectedPerSpec: parallel defer fetches must not race on shared mutable state; concurrent resolution must be data-race free under -race.
rootCauseArea: github.com/wundergraph/graphql-go-tools/v2 pkg/engine/resolve/loader.go + astjson (engine dependency, NOT in this repo)

reproQuery: { user(id:"u1"){ id recommendedArticles{ id title } ... @defer { recommendedArticles{ id title ... @defer { heroImageUrl stats{ views } reviews{ id rating } } } } } }
altRepro: { podcast(id:"p1"){ id host{ id displayName } ... @defer(label:"hostDeep"){ host{ id ... @defer(label:"hostReviews"){ reviews{ id rating body } } ... @defer(label:"hostRecs"){ recommendedArticles{ id title } } } } } }
observed: valid query (normal=200, 0 errors, full data). Defer mode emits ONLY the initial frame {data, pending:[...], hasNext:true} then CLOSES the multipart stream (--graphql--) with NO incremental/completed frames and NO hasNext:false terminal. Announced pending deferred content is never delivered -> client hangs + data loss.
expectedPerSpec: every announced pending id must be delivered+completed and the stream must end with exactly one hasNext:false frame.
trigger: nested defer (defer-within-defer) where a field is selected BOTH outside and inside the defer (overlapping selection across the defer boundary).
